summary: Demand evidence agents completed, but readiness still has blockers.

demand_status.summary: Demand is not PRD-ready; missing slots, blockers, assumptions, or evidence remain.

triage.explanation: Careful route required by hard triggers; factual or risky claims need evidence before PRD.

problem: Existing YOLO provider dispatch API/state handling must not drift between Claude, Codex, and custom provider assumptions in demand evidence mode.
status_quo: YOLO demand evidence dispatch exists in a dirty working tree and must be stress-tested with real Claude -p agents.
desired_outcome: Provider configuration preserves full tool capability under harness boundary and avoids cross-provider model leakage.
scope_out: Do not intentionally modify target project files during evidence gathering.
constraints: Full tools may be available; harness boundary audit must detect any target-project mutation.
acceptance_criteria: Provider configuration preserves full tool capability under harness boundary and avoids cross-provider model leakage.
risks: A provider-specific assumption can silently weaken dispatch or break real agent calls.

protocol.objective: Find primary project facts for the requested demand without editing files.
protocol.prompt: Read only the relevant files, configs, logs, docs, and current implementation. Return claims with exact paths/lines or source summaries. Separate assumptions from facts.

evidence_tasks.reason: Factual project claims need primary evidence.

protocol.objective: Independently verify high-risk factual claims and challenge the explorer result.
protocol.prompt: Use independent reads or sources. Confirm, weaken, or contradict each claim. Any conflict must become a blocker instead of being averaged away.
protocol.required_when: evidence_policy === cross_check or claim risk is high

protocol.objective: Check whether gathered evidence is enough to support PRD readiness and acceptance criteria.
protocol.prompt: Verify that no assumption is presented as fact, evidence paths/sources are usable, and unresolved missing data is reflected as blockers or assumptions.

evidence_tasks.reason: Verify blockers and assumptions are not promoted to facts.

state.next_action: Run explorer + cross-checker + verifier before treating project claims as facts.

agent_results.claim: YOLO provider dispatch API/state handling has recent changes in evidence-dispatch.ts and provider-adapter.ts to support demand evidence mode with boundary enforcement and provider-specific configuration isolation. Cross-provider model leakage prevention exists but requires stress testing with real agents.

evidence.summary: settingsValue function added to handle JSON settings strings vs file paths for Claude provider configuration
evidence.why: New capability for inline settings in evidence mode

evidence.summary: Claude provider now supports claude_tools, claude_allowed_tools, claude_disallowed_tools, claude_disable_slash_commands, claude_no_session_persistence, and max_budget_usd flags
evidence.why: Tool profile management for evidence mode boundary enforcement

evidence.summary: Codex provider includes explicit Claude model leak prevention: if model is empty and ai.model contains claude, ai.model is cleared to avoid cross-provider model confusion
evidence.why: Cross-provider model leakage mitigation

evidence.summary: buildBoundarySnapshot function creates file digests before and after agent execution to detect mutations, using git ls-files when available or walkFiles fallback
evidence.why: Core harness boundary enforcement mechanism

evidence.summary: diffBoundarySnapshots compares before/after snapshots to detect added, modified, or deleted files for boundary violation detection
evidence.why: Mutation detection logic for boundary enforcement

evidence.summary: executionConfig function normalizes provider settings including agent_tool_profile mapping to research/full/boundary for Claude, and sets claude_permission_mode to bypassPermissions
evidence.why: Provider-specific configuration normalization for evidence mode

evidence.summary: runDemandEvidenceDispatchRuntime spawns agents, captures results, audits boundary changes, and updates readiness with blockers for mutations or missing external evidence
evidence.why: Complete evidence dispatch lifecycle with boundary enforcement

evidence.summary: Test codex dispatch does not inherit claude default model or force read-only sandbox, verifying provider isolation
evidence.why: Provider isolation test coverage

evidence.summary: Test claude dispatch keeps default tools available and relies on boundary enforcement instead of tool restriction
evidence.why: Boundary enforcement over tool restriction approach validation

evidence.summary: Test boundary blocks project file mutation even when agent results pass, verifying boundary enforcement actually works
evidence.why: Boundary enforcement effectiveness test

evidence.summary: Claude provider demand dispatch does not use allowedTools/disallowedTools/tools restrictions; safety comes from prompt contract, artifact root, boundary snapshots, and readiness blockers
evidence.why: Design rationale for boundary-based safety vs tool restrictions

evidence.summary: demandRequestsExternalResearch and externalEvidencePresent functions detect when demand requires external research and verify scope=external evidence exists
evidence.why: External evidence validation for demand compliance

evidence.summary: buildDemandEvidenceAgentPrompt generates protocol-specific prompts with boundary rules, scope requirements, and external research instructions based on tool profile
evidence.why: Agent prompt generation with boundary and scope contract enforcement

evidence.summary: Recent dispatch execution shows clean boundary with project_mutation: clean and zero changes, proving boundary enforcement works in practice
evidence.why: Real-world evidence that boundary enforcement is functioning

evidence.summary: Provider runs show successful Claude provider execution with json_repaired: false for all three agent roles, indicating stable JSON parsing
evidence.why: Evidence of stable provider execution in evidence mode

assumptions: The git working tree with 55 modified files includes intentional changes for evidence dispatch and is not a symptom of provider configuration drift
Cross-provider model leakage prevention in provider-adapter.ts line 522-523 is sufficient but may not cover all edge cases
Stress test artifacts in .yolo/stress/ indicate previous testing but current demand claims these tests need to be run with real Claude -p agents
risks: A provider-specific assumption in executionConfig or buildProviderInvocation could silently weaken dispatch or break real agent calls if provider isolation is incomplete
Tool profile flags (claude_tools, claude_allowed_tools, claude_disallowed_tools) may have different behavior across Claude versions or configurations
Boundary enforcement relies on file system state; concurrent modifications or timing issues could produce false negatives or positives
The settingsValue function may not handle all valid JSON settings formats, leading to configuration errors
Cross-provider model leakage prevention only clears ai.model for Codex when it contains 'claude', which may not catch all model leakage scenarios
missing: Stress test results using real Claude -p agents to verify provider isolation and boundary enforcement under load
Evidence that tool profile flags (claude_tools, claude_allowed_tools, claude_disallowed_tools) preserve full tool capability under harness boundary
Verification that cross-provider model leakage prevention covers all edge cases beyond Claude model string detection
Testing of boundary enforcement with concurrent project modifications or unusual file system states

result.notes: Provider configuration includes boundary enforcement, provider isolation measures, and tool profile management. Evidence from code, tests, docs, and recent dispatch execution shows the implementation is functioning as designed. Cross-provider model leakage prevention exists but may benefit from additional stress testing with real agents to validate all edge cases.

agent_results.claim: Cross-check CRITICAL BLOCKER: Explorer misattributed cross-provider model leakage prevention code to wrong file. The code is NOT in provider-adapter.ts lines 521-523 as claimed (file is only 217 lines). Actual location is evidence-dispatch.ts lines 521-523. This is a factual error that must be corrected. Other evidence locations verified correctly: boundary enforcement, provider isolation, and tool profile management exist as documented in correct files.

evidence.summary: Full provider-adapter.ts file read confirms it is only 217 lines long. Lines 521-523 DO NOT EXIST in this file.
evidence.why: Disproves explorer evidence claim about cross-provider model leakage location

evidence.summary: ACTUAL location of cross-provider model leakage prevention: if ((ai.provider || ai.executor) === codex) { if (!model && !clean(ai.codex_model) && /\bclaude\b/i.test(clean(ai.model))) ai.model = ''; }
evidence.why: Corrects critical file attribution error in explorer evidence

evidence.summary: settingsValue function confirmed at correct location: handles JSON strings vs file paths for Claude provider settings
evidence.why: Verifies evidence location accuracy for this claim

evidence.summary: Claude provider tool flags confirmed: claude_tools, claude_allowed_tools, claude_disallowed_tools, claude_disable_slash_commands, claude_no_session_persistence, max_budget_usd
evidence.why: Verifies evidence location accuracy for tool profile management

evidence.summary: buildBoundarySnapshot confirmed: creates file digests using git ls-files or walkFiles fallback for boundary enforcement
evidence.why: Verifies boundary enforcement implementation exists as claimed

evidence.summary: diffBoundarySnapshots confirmed: compares before/after snapshots to detect added, modified, deleted files
evidence.why: Verifies mutation detection logic accuracy

evidence.summary: Recent dispatch artifact confirms clean boundary with project_mutation: clean and zero changes
evidence.why: Verifies real-world evidence that boundary enforcement functions correctly

evidence.summary: Provider runs show json_repaired: false for all three agent roles, indicating stable JSON parsing in evidence mode
evidence.why: Confirms provider execution stability claim

evidence.summary: Test verified: codex dispatch does not inherit claude default model or force read-only sandbox, confirming provider isolation
evidence.why: Verifies test coverage for provider isolation claims

evidence.summary: Doctrine confirmed: Claude provider dispatch does not use allowedTools/disallowedTools restrictions; safety from prompt contract, artifact root, boundary snapshots, and readiness blockers
evidence.why: Verifies design rationale documentation accuracy

assumptions: The explorer's file attribution error for cross-provider model leakage prevention may be a copy-paste mistake or confusion between files, but this reduces confidence in evidence gathering accuracy
risks: If the explorer misattributed one critical piece of evidence to the wrong file, other evidence locations may also contain similar attribution errors that were not detected in this cross-check
The cross-provider model leakage prevention being in evidence-dispatch.ts instead of provider-adapter.ts means it may not apply to all provider invocation paths, only to evidence dispatch runtime
Future evidence gathering based on this explorer result could propagate the incorrect file location to downstream agents or documentation
missing: Verification that cross-provider model leakage prevention in evidence-dispatch.ts applies to all provider execution paths or only to evidence dispatch runtime
Explanation of why cross-provider model leakage prevention is in evidence-dispatch.ts instead of provider-adapter.ts where other provider-specific logic exists

result.notes: CRITICAL BLOCKER: Explorer evidence claim #3 contains a factual error. Cross-provider model leakage prevention code is NOT in provider-adapter.ts lines 521-523 as claimed. The file is only 217 lines long. Actual location is evidence-dispatch.ts lines 521-523. This file attribution error must be corrected before PRD readiness can proceed. All other evidence locations verified accurately, but this critical error undermines confidence in the evidence gathering process.

agent_results.claim: Explorer evidence contains a CRITICAL factual error that must be corrected before PRD readiness. Cross-provider model leakage prevention code was misattributed to wrong file and line numbers. Cross-checker correctly identified this blocker. Evidence gathering process failed quality control, but remaining evidence after correction is sufficient to support PRD with updated file locations.

evidence.summary: File is only 217 lines long; explorer claim of lines 521-523 is impossible
evidence.why: Confirms factual error in evidence location

evidence.summary: Actual location of cross-provider model leakage prevention code confirmed by cross-checker
evidence.why: Corrects the file attribution error

evidence.summary: Recent dispatch execution artifact shows clean boundary with project_mutation: clean and zero changes
evidence.why: Verifies boundary enforcement is functioning correctly in practice

evidence.summary: Provider runs confirm stable Claude provider execution with json_repaired: false across all agent roles
evidence.why: Evidence that provider execution is stable in evidence mode

evidence.summary: Boundary enforcement implementation (buildBoundarySnapshot and diffBoundarySnapshots) exists and is tested
evidence.why: Core mechanism for detecting target-project mutations

evidence.summary: Design rationale documented: Claude provider safety comes from prompt contract, artifact root, boundary snapshots, not tool restrictions
evidence.why: Explains why evidence mode uses boundary enforcement instead of tool restrictions

evidence.summary: Test verifies boundary blocks project file mutation even when agent results pass
evidence.why: Confirms boundary enforcement effectiveness is tested

assumptions: The explorer's file attribution error is an isolated mistake rather than systemic evidence gathering failure
Cross-provider model leakage prevention in evidence-dispatch.ts applies to evidence dispatch runtime; other provider paths may need separate handling
The dirty git working tree with 55 modified files represents intentional evidence dispatch work, not provider configuration drift
risks: If evidence location errors exist beyond the one caught by cross-checker, PRD decisions may be based on incorrect file references
Cross-provider model leakage prevention being in evidence-dispatch.ts instead of provider-adapter.ts means it may not apply to all provider execution contexts
Stress testing with real Claude -p agents has not been completed and may reveal additional edge cases in provider isolation

result.notes: BLOCKER: Explorer evidence item #3 contains CRITICAL factual error. Cross-provider model leakage prevention code is NOT in provider-adapter.ts lines 521-523 (file is only 217 lines). Correct location is evidence-dispatch.ts lines 521-523. Cross-checker correctly identified this error. The evidence gathering process failed basic validation, but after correcting the file location, sufficient evidence exists from code, tests, docs, and artifacts to support PRD readiness for boundary enforcement and provider isolation. Stress testing with real agents remains outstanding but is not a PRD blocker. VERDICT: Blocked until evidence file locations are corrected, then proceed.
